> _Verbatim cross-reference verdict (ours vs orsc vs Jagex ground truth) from the render-fidelity-cross-reference workflow (2026-06-01)._

## Cross-reference adjudication — walls / scenery / diagonal doors

Arbiter = the Jagex rev-235 clean decompile (`decompiled/normalized-clean/k.java` = World, plus the deob `src/client/world/World.java` and `src/client/Mudclient.java`). Three sources compared per behavior: OURS (working-tree render/*.go at HEAD), ORSC (`origin/main:render/orsc/*`), JAGEX (clean k.java / deob).

### Evidence summary

**1. Wall front/back colour via method422 (not a stone/wood heuristic)**
- JAGEX `k.java method422` (deob World.java:770-784): `frontColour = wallFrontColour_v_a[id]` (modelVar2 / FrontDeco), `backColour = wallBackColour_Jk[id]` (modelVar3 / BackDeco), passed **verbatim** to `createFace(4, …, frontColour, backColour, false)`. Front and back are independent; a value `<0` is a flat 5:5:5 colour, `>=0` is a texture id.
- OURS `boundary.go:84-126` (`resolveFill` + `addWall`): ports method422 verbatim — `front,_ = resolveFill(def.FrontDeco)`, `back,_ = resolveFill(def.BackDeco)`; `<0` → flat colour, `>=0` → textured span if a texel buffer exists else a baked per-id flat colour. The old stone/wood `Unknown`/name heuristic was dropped. Front≠back preserved. This was a fix to OUR prior bug, not a Jagex divergence.
- ORSC `walls.go insertWall` (origin/main render/orsc/walls.go:48-66): `model.insertFace(4, …, int32(def.FrontDeco), int32(def.BackDeco))` — verbatim front/back from the def, independent. `raster.go fillFace:668-678` resolves `fill>=0` as a real texture (reads the archive), `fill<0` as flat colour. Matches OpenRSC `insertWallIntoModel` (World.java:1425-1435, `getModelVar2()`/`getModelVar3()`).
- WINNER: **agree** (both ours and orsc match Jagex method422). The only difference is the no-archive degenerate path: ORSC clamps an unavailable texture id to texture 0; OURS falls back to a baked per-id flat colour (`textureFill`). With the archive present both paint real texels. Not a colour-table divergence.

**2. id-74 vertical lift (-480)**
- JAGEX deob Mudclient.java:6229-6231 (and 4296-4298): `if (objType == 74) model.a(0, 0, -480, true)` = `translate(x=0,y=0,z=-480)`. GameModel.translate does `baseZ += z`, and apply() does `translateVertices(0, baseZ, baseY, baseX)` so **baseZ maps onto the vertical Y axis** (GameModel.java:838-847, 1200-1201). -Y is up ⇒ sails lift 480 up onto the mill tower.
- OURS `scenery.go:147-149`: `if loc.DefID == 74 { cy -= 480 }` where `cy` is the vertical Y anchor. Correct axis, correct magnitude.
- ORSC: **no id-74 handling anywhere** (grep of all 17 orsc files = 0 hits for `74`/`-480`/`sail`/`windmill`). `PlaceScenery` (world.go:655-663) does only `Orient(0,dir*32,0)` + `Translate(cx,-getElevation,cz)` with no per-id lift. Windmill sails render flat on the ground.
- WINNER: **ours**. This is a genuine ORSC gap (the Jagex/OpenRSC special-case is absent).

**3. Diagonal scenery objects / doors (band 48001..59999) via World.addModels**
- JAGEX deob World.java:826-854 (`addModels`) and clean k.java:1930-2010: for every tile with `48000 < getWallDiagonal < 60000`: `objectId = diag - 48001`; `dir = getTileDirection`; footprint `(w,hgt)` from `objectWidth_ub_g`/`objectHeight_f_f` with the dir-0/4 swap; clone prototype; translate to footprint centre `cx=128*(w+2x)/2, cy=128*(hgt+2y)/2` at `-getElevation(cx,cy)`; `orient(0,-999999,0, dir*32)` (dir*32 → **roll**); `scene.addModel`; `setLight(48,48,-10,…,-50,-50)`; then clear the id over the footprint. This is a distinct pass from straight walls (which method422 builds, band 1..23999).
- OURS `diagobj.go BuildDiagonalObjects`: ports addModels exactly — band `>48000 && <60000`, `objectId = diag-48001`, `dir = TileDirection`, footprint+dir-0/4 swap delegated to the verified `PlaceScenery`, footprint-centre translate, `dir*32`→roll (`Rotate(0,0,dir*32)`, matching `orient(…, roll=dir*32)`), `-elevation` ground-snap, footprint-clear on a local grid snapshot (no shared-landscape mutation). Real-def path routes through `PlaceScenery`; synthetic wood-leaf fallback only with no archive.
- ORSC: **no addModels port anywhere**. `walls.go wallPass/doorPass` handle only `<12000` and `>12000 && <24000` (diagonal WALLS); `roofs.go` reads the diagonal grid only in the `<24000` ranges for ridge orientation; `world.go` colour-split branches gate on `< 24000`; `scenery_place.go` is driven by OpenRSC's `SceneryLocs.json` and its own comment (scenery_place.go:11-13) explicitly says it uses SceneryLocs "rather than the landscape's 48000+ inline markers (which OpenRSC superseded with SceneryLocs)." So the 48000+ inline diagonal-object/door band builds **zero geometry** in orsc.
- Lineage: OpenRSC's own `World.addLoginScreenModels` (Client_Base/.../three/World.java:168-193) DOES handle `>48000 && <60000` (`diagWall = getWallDiagonal - 48001`). ORSC's three/ port dropped it in favour of server-fed SceneryLocs. So ORSC diverges from BOTH the Jagex client and its own OpenRSC lineage for this band.
- WINNER: **ours**. Genuine ORSC gap.

### Side-finding (flagged, outside the 3 assigned items): wall-visible gate
The Jagex standing-wall loop gates each wall on `wallVisible_lb_Tb[id] == 0 || memberWorld` (deob World.java:1039/1048/1057/1067; `lb.Tb` = GameData wallObjectInvisible flag). ORSC `wallPass` gates on `def.Unknown != 0` (skip → built separately in `doorPass`); OpenRSC's `getUnknown()`/`Unknown` is the same openable/invisible-boundary flag, so ORSC's split-pass approach matches Jagex semantics (openable doors are drawn as separate panels). OURS deliberately builds all non-zero wall ids with no gate (documented finding #9, PROPOSED/intentional-for-the-bot). This is the one place ORSC tracks Jagex more closely than OURS — noted for completeness; not in the assigned adjudication scope and OURS is an intentional design choice, so no action.

## VERDICTS

| behavior | OUR logic (file:line) | ORSC logic (file:line) | JAGEX ground truth (file:line) | WINNER | ACTION | evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Wall front/back colour via method422 (per-material colour table, front≠back, verbatim) | boundary.go:84-101 `resolveFill`; :115-125 `addWall` front/back | render/orsc/walls.go:48-66 `insertWall` verbatim FrontDeco/BackDeco; raster.go:668-678 flat-vs-texture | deob World.java:770-784 `method422` (`wallFrontColour_v_a`/`wallBackColour_Jk` verbatim); clean k.java method422 | agree | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT | Both pass def front/back colour verbatim (matches OpenRSC `getModelVar2/3`, World.java:1431-1435). OURS fixed our own old stone/wood heuristic; ORSC never had it. Only diff: no-archive degenerate path (ours bakes a flat colour, orsc clamps texid→0) — not a colour-table divergence. |
| id-74 windmill-sail vertical lift (-480 on Y) | scenery.go:147-149 `if loc.DefID==74 { cy -= 480 }` | (absent — no `74`/`-480` in any of the 17 render/orsc files) | deob Mudclient.java:6229-6231 & 4296-4298 `if(objType==74) model.a(0,0,-480,true)`; baseZ→Y via GameModel.java:838-847,1200-1201 | ours | PORT-OURS-TO-ORSC-GAP | ORSC's PlaceScenery (world.go:655-663) does Orient+Translate only, no per-id lift → sails lie flat. Genuine gap; forward-port `-480` for id 74. |
| Diagonal scenery objects/doors band 48001..59999 (World.addModels) | render/diagobj.go (whole) `BuildDiagonalObjects`; wired render.go:330-348 | (absent — orsc only handles diagonal WALLS `<24000`; scenery_place.go:11-13 explicitly skips "48000+ inline markers" for SceneryLocs.json) | deob World.java:826-854 `addModels`; clean k.java:1930-2010 (`48000<d<60000`, `objectId=d-48001`, footprint centre, `orient(…,roll=dir*32)`, footprint-clear) | ours | PORT-OURS-TO-ORSC-GAP | ORSC builds ZERO geometry for the 48000+ band. Diverges from Jagex AND from its own OpenRSC lineage (OpenRSC World.java:168-193 handles `>48000 && <60000`). Forward-port diagobj.go (drive from the landscape diag grid, not just SceneryLocs). |
| (side) diagonal-object orientation: dir*32 → ROLL, not yaw | scenery.go:156 / diagobj.go synthetic `Rotate(0,0,dir*32)` | n/a (band unhandled) | clean k.java:1960 `var9.g(0,-999999,0,dir*32)` = orient(yaw=0,_,pitch=0,roll=dir*32) | ours | (part of diagobj port) | OUR roll mapping matches the clean `orient` roll arg; static-scenery path likewise uses roll (Mudclient.java:4292 `f(0,-31616,orient*32,0)`). Faithful. |
| (side, out of scope) standing-wall visible gate | boundary.go: no gate (builds all ids) — finding #9 PROPOSED/intentional | walls.go:73-81 `wallPass` skips `Unknown!=0`; doorPass builds them separately | deob World.java:1039/1048/1057/1067 `wallVisible_lb_Tb[id]==0 || memberWorld` | orsc | (no action — OURS intentional) | ORSC's Unknown-split matches Jagex's invisible-flag gate + separate door panels. OURS intentionally builds closed doors for the bot; left as-is per RENDER_FIDELITY_FINDINGS #9. Flagged for honesty, not in assigned scope. |

Net: all three assigned items confirmed. Item 1 (wall colours) — ORSC is correct and AGREES with Jagex; OUR fix was repairing our own prior heuristic (redundant relative to orsc, both now correct). Items 2 (id-74 lift) and 3 (diagonal-object band 48001..59999) are **genuine ORSC gaps** — ORSC matches neither the Jagex clean decompile nor its OpenRSC lineage — and OUR fixes should be forward-ported (PORT-OURS-TO-ORSC-GAP). No NEEDS-RUNTIME-DIFF for these three: the source evidence is unambiguous.

Relevant files: OURS — `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/render/boundary.go`, `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/render/scenery.go`, `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/render/diagobj.go`, `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/render/model.go`. ORSC — `origin/main:render/orsc/{walls.go,scenery_place.go,world.go,roofs.go,raster.go}`. JAGEX — `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/src/client/world/World.java` (method422:770-784, addModels:826-854, wall loop:1036-1074), `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/src/client/Mudclient.java:6229-6231,4296-4298`, `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/src/client/scene/GameModel.java:816-857,1200-1201`, clean `/home/free/code/rsc-hacking/westworld/reference/rsc-client/decompiled/normalized-clean/k.java:1930-2010`.
